title: crotalaria barkae subsp. teitensis
intro: Crotalaria barkae subsp. teitensis, also known as Teit yellow pea, is a plant species within the legume family Fabaceae, native to East Africa, particularly in Kenya. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antiseptic and antibacterial activities, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Crotalaria barkae subsp. teitensis include its potential to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat various ailments, and its leaves and stems can be used to make a decoction.
